chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. C.P.No.D-2420 of 2015 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 16.12.2025. Mr. Dinesh Kumar, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri Assistant Advocate General, Sindh. = This petition was disposed of vide order dated 13.03.2018, with the following terms:- “ Petitioner and respondent No.6 present file a joint statement requiring the disposal of instant petition in view of terms as mentioned in the said statement wherein it is settled that respondent No.6 will pay outstanding amount of Rs.36,11,000/- to petitioner by mean of instalments of Rs.1,25,000/-per month as well as his monthly pension.” Today, learned A.A.G has filed statement of respondents No.4 and 5, the contents whereof reproduced hereinbelow:- “The Respondent No: 4 & 5 (Administrator Market Committee Shahdadpur and Secretary Market Committee Shahdadpur) respectfully submits as under: 01. That the Market Committee Shahdadpur is U/s 14 of Agriculture Produce Market Act 1939 and Rule 1940 Corporate Body and no any aid / funds are received from the Government side and Market Committee is collected fee on Agriculture Produced. 02. That the Applicant Petitioner remained on the Post of Secretary Market Committee Shahdadpur from 27-06-2012 to 19-12-2012, only 5 months and 22 days the Market Committee Shahdadpur was already paid the entire amount to the petitioner pensionery benefits by the respondents as under: i) Total Dues of Pension benefits 31-12-2025 Rs.11288975/= ii) Paid to the Petitioner Rs: 9425851/= iii) Out Standing Amount Rs: 1863124/= 03. That the current position of the Market Committee Shahdadpur is very serious due to non availability of funds and the no any recovery staff available except Naib Qasid, Chowkidar and Sweeper, further it is submitted that the except the petitioner one employees namely Muhammad Ishtiq retired Secretary has filed CPD No:724 of 2025 in Honorable High Court of Sindh Mirpurkhas and other remaining employees were remaining the pensionery benefits Rs: 27353704/-and also salaries of the Regular Staff Rs: 24539541/- 04.

That the Applicant Petitioner remained on the Post of Secretary Market Committee Shahdadpur from 27-06-2012 to 19-12-2012, only 5 months and 22 days the Market Committee Shahdadpur was already paid the entire amount to the petitioner pensionery benefits by the respondents as under: i) Total Dues of Pension benefits 31-12-2025 Rs.11288975/= ii) Paid to the Petitioner Rs: 9425851/= iii) Out Standing Amount Rs: 1863124/= 03. That the current position of the Market Committee Shahdadpur is very serious due to non availability of funds and the no any recovery staff available except Naib Qasid, Chowkidar and Sweeper, further it is submitted that the except the petitioner one employees namely Muhammad Ishtiq retired Secretary has filed CPD No:724 of 2025 in Honorable High Court of Sindh Mirpurkhas and other remaining employees were remaining the pensionery benefits Rs: 27353704/-and also salaries of the Regular Staff Rs: 24539541/- 04. That as per Notification on dated 4-6-2012, the petitioner remain posted in deferent Market Committees, he is collect his pension benefits from other Market Committees and deposited in the account of Market Committee Shahdadpur but he could not collect the pension contribution and not deposited in the account of Market Committee Shahdadpur, That t paid paid Rs:100,000/ Cheque No 107342200% National Bank of Pakistan Branch dated: 23-12-2025, the Market committee will pay Rs: 30000/- every month in remaining amount to the petitioner. 05. That respondent No 4 and 5 respectively submitted that looking to the poor financial position pay the monthly pension availability of funds Market Committee Shahdadpur further pay the remaining amount to the petitioner coming Cotton season. In view of the above condition this Honorable Court may kindly be pleased to disposed off petition long with pending application accordingly.” Learned A.A.G submits that he has brought cheque bearing No.1073422090 dated 23.12.2025 amounting to Rs.100,000/= which has been handed over to the counsel for petitioner for onward submission in the account of petitioner. However, learned counsel for the petitioner seeks time to go through the submission so made in the matter. Time is allowed. To come-up on 22.01.2025. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmed/Pa
